html:
A Morsel is a meat food item that is dropped by Rabbits, Redbirds, Crows, Snowbirds, Smallbirds, Baby Beefalo (as Baby and Toddler), Splumonkeys, Moleworms, Buzzards, Prime Apes, and Pengulls when they are killed. A Morsel can be used in several Crock Pot recipes as a meat filler, and can be dried on a Drying Rack for 1 day to produce a Small Jerky. Like other meats, a morsel can be eaten by a Pig to have them follow the player, or can be given to the Pig King in exchange for a Gold nugget. It has a meat value of 0.5.

Cooked Morsel

A Cooked Morsel is a Morsel, Rabbit, or captured Bird that has been cooked over a Campfire, Fire Pit or Star. It can also be used in various Crock Pot recipes. Mobs that ordinarily drop Morsels that die from being set on fire will drop a Cooked Morsel instead. Like other Meats, a Cooked Morsel can be given to a Pig to have them follow the player, or to the Pig King in exchange for a Gold Nugget.

Small Jerky

Small Jerky is created by using a Drying Rack to dry an uncooked Morsel, Fish, Eel, Drumstick or Frog Legs for 1 day, or a Batilisk Wing for 2 days. The Small Jerky has an extremely low rate of spoilage (taking 20 days) and restores 12.5 Hunger, 8 Health and 10 Sanity when eaten. This makes it an excellent item for keeping sanity high, particularly in winter, if one has a stock of rabbits or other constant supply of meat.

Jerky can also be used in the Crock Pot in place of meat or morsels with no negative side effects. Thus, 2 Meat and 2 Morsels turned into 2 pieces of Jerky and 2 pieces of Small Jerky can be used to make Meaty Stew.

Unharvested Small Jerky will not start to spoil as long it's left on the Drying Rack, making it possible to keep it fresh for an indefinite amount of time.

This food can be useful for recovering from the effects of Woodie's Werebeaver transformation, though it is significantly weaker than Jerky, thus requiring a lot more Small Jerky for such a recovery.

Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants and Shipwrecked DLCs, Small Jerky will begin to spoil immediately upon creation on a Drying Rack, regardless of whether it is harvested or not.

Tips

  • As Small Jerky is not technically cooked, Wagstaff will still suffer a 3 penalty from consuming it, bringing the total healing down to 5 . This makes Small Jerky inefficient for healing Wagstaff, at least compared to other meals such as Fishsticks or Honey Ham.

Gallery

  • Original HD Morsel icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Original HD Cooked Morsel icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

“A tiny chunk of a dead animal.”
–Wilson, when examining a Morsel.
“I could make this better with FIRE!”
–Willow, when examining a Morsel.
“I need more meat!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Morsel.
“It smells like tiny fear.”
–Wendy, when examining a Morsel.
“IT TASTES LIKE REVENGE.”
–WX-78, when examining a Morsel.
“It's a small, raw piece of meat.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Morsel.
“I wish this were bigger.”
–Woodie, when examining a Morsel.
“This is barely a mouthful.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Morsel.
“A tiny bit of protein. I will need to cook it though.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Morsel.
“A nice meaty snack.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Morsel.
“A couple more and we will have a meal!”
–Webber, when examining a Morsel.
“Mmmmmeat!”
–Walani, when examining a Morsel.
“Fresh protein!”
–Warly, when examining a Morsel.
“Tis' small, but tis' meat.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Morsel.
“'TIS BUT A TOKEN O' MEATS”
–Wilba, when examining a Morsel.
“Little belly thing”
–Wormwood, when examining a Morsel.
“Hardly any meat on that bone to put meat on your bones.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Morsel.
“Looks like grub to me.”
–Winona, when examining a Morsel.
“Poor little creature.”
–Wortox, when examining a Morsel.
“Glurgh...”
–Wurt, when examining a Morsel.
“Just a little meat.”
–Walter, when examining a Morsel.
“A little meat is better than none.”
–Wanda, when examining a Morsel.
A Morsel is a meat food item that is dropped by Rabbits, Redbirds, Crows, Snowbirds, Smallbirds, Baby Beefalo (as Baby and Toddler), Splumonkeys, Moleworms, Buzzards, Prime Apes, and Pengulls when they are killed. "cookedsmallmeat"
“Now I don't have to worry about getting worms!”
–Wilson, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“I'll need more than appetizers if I'm going to survive.”
–Willow, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“It is even smaller cooked!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“Fire has purified it.”
–Wendy, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“IT TASTES LIKE BURNT REVENGE.”
–WX-78, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“It is slightly more appetizing when cooked.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“That'll just make me hungrier!”
–Woodie, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“Well, at least it's not moving anymore.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“Based on past experiments, I deduce I can eat this.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“Yum, yum, meat snacks.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“Meat treat.”
–Webber, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“A tasty morsel.”
–Walani, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“Could use sea salt...”
–Warly, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“'Tis but a morsel.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“'TIS TEENSIE YUMS!”
–Wilba, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“Little belly stuff”
–Wormwood, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“The snacking meat.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“Well, a morsel's a morsel.”
–Winona, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“At least it smells better now.”
–Wortox, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“Won't eat it, flurp.”
–Wurt, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“A little meat is better than none.”
–Walter, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
“It's at least enough to hold me over for the time being.”
–Wanda, when examining a Cooked Morsel.
A Cooked Morsel is a Morsel, Rabbit, or captured Bird that has been cooked over a Campfire, Fire Pit or Star. It can also be used in various Crock Pot recipes. Mobs that ordinarily drop Morsels that die from being set on fire will drop a Cooked Morsel instead. Like other Meats, a Cooked Morsel can be given to a Pig to have them follow the player, or to the Pig King in exchange for a Gold Nugget.

–Wilson
“Chewy, but satisfying.”

“Look like leather, taste like meat.”

“Meat, turned dry and delicious.”

–Wickerbottom
“Just a bite.”
–Woodie
“My teeth are getting too old for this.”
–Maxwell
“Ah, this should tide me over for a while.”
–Wagstaff
“A small prövisiön för a löng campaign.”
–Wigfrid
“It'll keep longer this way.”
–Webber
“A treat that travels well.”
–Walani
“Could use a teriyaki glaze...”
–Warly
“Jolly jerky!”
–Woodlegs
“NUM NUMS FOR WILBA”
–Wilba
“Chewy”
–Wormwood
“Dry. Chewy. Ready to be eaten.”
–Wheeler
“Meat to go.”
–Winona
“Not to my taste.”
–Wortox
“Glargh, it even worse now.”
–Wurt
“Barely a bite of jerky.”
–Walter
“A bite of tough, dry meat.”
–Wanda

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Thịt Nhỏ là một loại thức ăn có thịt rơi ra bởi Thỏ, Chim Hồng, Quạ, Chim Tuyết, Chim Nhỏ, Bò Lai Con (khi còn là Trẻ Sơ Sinh và Trẻ Mới Biết Đi), Khỉ Khỉ, Giun Chũi, Diều, Khỉ Prime và Cánh Cụt Biển khi chúng bị giết. Thịt Nhỏ có thể được sử dụng trong một số công thức nấu Nồi Hầm làm chất độn thịt, và có thể phơi khô trên Giá Phơi trong 1 ngày để tạo ra Thịt Khô Nhỏ. Giống như các loại thịt khác, một miếng thịt có thể được Lợn ăn để chúng đi theo người chơi hoặc có thể đưa cho Vua Lợn để đổi lấy Vàng cốm. Nó có giá trị thịt là 0,5.
